_scoring_notes.kinetic_formula: 40% intercept rate (scaled 90-100% → 0-100pts) + 20% absolute debris penalty (100 - debris×8) + 20% day-over-day trend (100 - delta×10, capped 0-100) + 20% 7-day avg trend (100 - delta×10, capped 0-100)
_scoring_notes.social_formula: Base 100. Deductions: -5 per NCEMA alert in last 24h, -15 US embassy Level 3 + suspended consular services, -10 UK FCDO advise against all but essential travel, -10 DXB partial ops, -5 AUH limited long-haul

social_inputs.usEmbassyAdvisory: Level 3 Reconsider Travel + suspended consular services
social_inputs.ukFCDOAdvisory: Advise against all but essential travel

sources: Gulf News: UAE air defences engage 5 ballistic missiles, 17 UAVs on March 24
Newland Chase: Middle East Conflict Situational Update March 24
US Embassy UAE ae.usembassy.gov: Level 3 Reconsider Travel, suspended consular services
UK FCDO: Advise against all but essential travel to UAE
News18 Dubai live blog March 24: NCEMA thunderstorm reassurance, morning alert wave
ARN News Centre: UAE air defences engage March 24
Hindustan Times Dubai live blog March 24
Iran threatening to mine Gulf and target regional power plants (AFP via News18)

dailyBriefing: On March 24, UAE air defences successfully intercepted all 5 ballistic missiles and 17 UAVs launched by Iran — a 100% intercept rate. Three debris incidents were reported: a fire in Deira from drone debris (contained by midday), a brief power outage in the Mirdif area, and debris near Al Quoz Industrial. The diplomatic situation deteriorated sharply as Trump's 48-hour Hormuz ultimatum expired today with Iran refusing to comply and threatening to mine all Gulf access routes and target regional power plants. Iran published target maps of UAE, Saudi, and Qatari power infrastructure. The UK FCDO upgraded its advisory to 'advise against all but essential travel' to the UAE. The US Embassy (Level 3: Reconsider Travel) has suspended all routine consular services. DXB is operating at partial capacity with Emirates and flydubai on scaled-down networks; AUH has limited long-haul services. UAE government stance remains normal life between alerts, with NCEMA issuing two alerts this morning (both resolved). Schools continue on distance learning through April 3 per KHDA extension.

schoolClosures.label: Distance Learning Until April 3
schoolClosures.guidance: KHDA extended distance learning for all private schools in Dubai until April 3. Schools plan to reopen April 6 subject to security conditions.

supermarketStock.label: Adequate — Air Freight Supplement
supermarketStock.guidance: Major chains (Carrefour, Lulu, Spinneys) remain stocked. Air-freight supplements continue for fresh produce. Prices rising on imported goods due to Hormuz disruption.

fuelAvailability.label: Normal Supply
fuelAvailability.guidance: ENOC and ADNOC stations fully operational across Dubai. No queues or shortages reported.
